Prompt: Create a private AWS S3 bucket for a trace demo. The bucket must block all public access, enable versioning, and enable server-side encryption using AES256.
Rego intent:
package terraform.validation

default valid = false

s3_bucket[resource] {
    resource := input.resource_changes[_]
    resource.mode == "managed"
    resource.type == "aws_s3_bucket"
}

versioning[resource] {
    resource := input.resource_changes[_]
    resource.mode == "managed"
    resource.type == "aws_s3_bucket_versioning"
    resource.change.after.versioning_configuration[0].status == "Enabled"
}

encryption[resource] {
    resource := input.resource_changes[_]
    resource.mode == "managed"
    resource.type == "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration"
    resource.change.after.rule[0].apply_server_side_encryption_by_default[0].sse_algorithm == "AES256"
}

public_access_block[resource] {
    resource := input.resource_changes[_]
    resource.mode == "managed"
    resource.type == "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block"
    resource.change.after.block_public_acls == true
    resource.change.after.block_public_policy == true
    resource.change.after.ignore_public_acls == true
    resource.change.after.restrict_public_buckets == true
}

valid {
    count(s3_bucket) > 0
    count(versioning) > 0
    count(encryption) > 0
    count(public_access_block) > 0
}
Intent: Creates a private S3 bucket with versioning, AES256 server-side encryption, and full public access blocking for a simple trace demo.
